title: Sheetla Chalisa
intro: Shri Sheetla Mata Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Goddess Sheetla Mata
description:
|| Shri Sheetala Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Jai jai Mata Sheetala, tumhi dharai jo dhyan.
Hoy vimal sheetal hriday, viksai buddhi bal gyan.
Ghat-ghat vasi Sheetala, sheetal prabha tumhar.
Sheetal chhaiya mein jhulayi, maiya palna daar.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai-jai-jai Shri Sheetala Bhavani.
Jai jag janani sakal gundhani.
Grih-grih shakti tumhari rajit.
Poorana sharadchandra samsajit.
Visphotak se jalat sharira.
Sheetal karat harat sab peeda.
Maat Sheetala tav shubhnama.
Sabke gadhe aavahi kama.
Shok hari Shankari Bhavani.
Baal-pranakshari sukh daani.
Shuchi marjani kalash karrajai.
Mastak tej surya sam sajai.
Chausath yogin sang mein gavain.
Veena taal mridang bajavain.
Nritya nath Bhairon dikhlavain.
Sahaj shesh Shiv paar na pavain.
Dhanya dhanya dhatri maharani.
Surnar muni tab suyash bakhani.
Jwala roop maha balkari.
Daitya ek visphotak bhari.
Ghar-ghar pravishat koi na rakshat.
Rog roop dhari balak bhakshat.
Hahakar machyo jagbhari.
Sakyo na jab sankat taari.
Tab maiyya dhari adbhut roopa.
Kar mein liye marjani soopa.
Visphotakahin pakdi kar leenho.
Moosal pramaan bahuvidhi keenho.
Bahut prakar vah vinati keenha.
Maiyya nahin bhal main kachu keenha.
Abnahi Matu kahugrih jaihaun.
Jahan apavitra vahi ghar rahi ho.
Aban bhagatan sheetal bhay jaihaun.
Visphotak bhay ghor nasaihaun.
Shri Sheetalahin bhaje kalyana.
Vachan satya bhashe bhagvana.
Poojan paath Matu jab kari hai.
Bhay aanand sakal dukh hari hai.
Visphotak bhay jihi grih bhai.
Bhajai Devi kahin yahi upai.
Kalash Sheetala ka sajavai.
Dwij se vidhivat paath karavai.
Tumhin Sheetala, jagki Mata.
Tumhin pita jag ki sukhdata.
Tumhin jagaddhatri sukhsevi.
Namo namami Sheetale Devi.
Namo sukhkarni dukh-harni.
Namo-namo jagtarani dharni.
Namo namo trilokya vandini.
Dukhdaridrak nikandini.
Shri Sheetala, Shedhala, Mahala.
Runalihrinani Matru Mandala.
Ho tum digambar tanudhari.
Shobhit panchnam asavari.
Rasabh, khar, Baisakh sunandan.
Gardabh durvakand nikandan.
Sumirat sang Sheetala mai, jahi sakal sukh door parai.
Galka, galgandadi juhoi.
Taakar mantra na aushadhi koi.
Ek Matu ji ka aaradhan.
Aur nahin koi hai sadhan.
Nishchay Matu sharan jo aavai.
Nirbhay man ichhit phal paavai.
Kodhi, nirmal kaya dharai.
Andha, drig nij drishti niharai.
Bandhya nari putra ko paavai.
Janm daridra dhani hoi jaavai.
Matu Sheetala ke gun gaavat.
Lakha mook ko chhand banaavat.
Yaame koi karai jani shanka.
Jag mein maiya ka hi danka.
Bhagat ‘Kamal’ Prabhudasa.
Tat Prayag se poorab pasa.
Gram Tiwari poor mam basa.
Kakra Ganga tat Durvasa.
Ab vilamb main tohi pukarat.
Matru kripa kau baat niharat.
Pada dwar sab aas lagayi.
Ab sudhi let Sheetala mai.

|| Doha ||
Yeh Chalisa Sheetala, paath kare jo koy.
Sapne dukh vyaape nahi, nit sab mangal hoy.
Bujhe sahasra vikrami shukl, bhaal bhal kintu.
Jag janani ka ye charit, rachit bhakti ras bintu.
meaning:
|| Shri Sheetala Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Jai jai Mata Sheetala, tumhin dharai jo dhyan.
(Victory to Mother Sheetala, whoever meditates on You.)
Hoy vimal sheetal hriday, viksai buddhi bal gyan.
(Their heart becomes pure and cool, intelligence, strength, and wisdom bloom.)
Ghat-ghat vasi Sheetala, sheetal prabha tumhar.
(Dweller of every heart, Mother Sheetala, your aura is cool and soothing.)
Sheetal chhaiya mein jhulayi, maiya palna daar.
(Mother, you rock us in your cool shade like a cradle.)

|| Chaupai ||
Jai-jai-jai Shri Sheetala Bhavani.
(Victory, victory, victory to Shri Sheetala Bhavani.)
Jai jag janani sakal gundhani.
(Victory to the Mother of the world, possessor of all virtues.)
Grih-grih shakti tumhari rajit.
(Your power shines in every home.)
Poorana sharadchandra samsajit.
(You are adorned like the full autumn moon.)
Visphotak se jalat sharira.
(The body burning from eruptive diseases/fevers.)
Sheetal karat harat sab peeda.
(You make it cool and remove all pain.)
Maat Sheetala tav shubhnama.
(Mother Sheetala, your name is auspicious.)
Sabke gadhe aavahi kama.
(You are useful to everyone in difficult times.)
Shok hari Shankari Bhavani.
(Remover of sorrows, O Shankari Bhavani.)
Baal-pranakshari sukh daani.
(Savior of children's lives, giver of happiness.)
Shuchi marjani kalash karrajai.
(The broom and the pot look beautiful in your hands.)
Mastak tej surya sam sajai.
(Your forehead shines with the brilliance of the sun.)
Chausath yogin sang mein gavain.
(The sixty-four Yoginis sing along with you.)
Veena taal mridang bajavain.
(They play the Veena, rhythms, and Mridangam.)
Nritya nath Bhairon dikhlavain.
(Lord Bhairon displays his dance.)
Sahaj shesh Shiv paar na pavain.
(Even Shesha and Shiva cannot fathom your depths.)
Dhanya dhanya dhatri maharani.
(Blessed, blessed are you, the great Queen/Nourisher.)
Surnar muni tab suyash bakhani.
(Gods, humans, and sages praise your glory.)
Jwala roop maha balkari.
(Your flame-like form is very powerful.)
Daitya ek visphotak bhari.
(There was a great demon named Visphotak.)
Ghar-ghar pravishat koi na rakshat.
(He entered every house, no one could protect themselves.)
Rog roop dhari balak bhakshat.
(Taking the form of disease, he consumed children.)
Hahakar machyo jagbhari.
(A great outcry spread through the world.)
Sakyo na jab sankat taari.
(When no one could avert this crisis.)
Tab maiyya dhari adbhut roopa.
(Then the Mother took an amazing form.)
Kar mein liye marjani soopa.
(Holding a broom and a winnowing basket in her hands.)
Visphotakahin pakdi kar leenho.
(She caught the demon Visphotak.)
Moosal pramaan bahuvidhi keenho.
(And punished him in many ways with a pestle.)
Bahut prakar vah vinati keenha.
(He prayed in many ways.)
Maiyya nahin bhal main kachu keenha.
(Saying, 'Mother, I have done nothing good'.)
Abnahi Matu kahugrih jaihaun.
(Now I will not go to anyone's house, Mother.)
Jahan apavitra vahi ghar rahi ho.
(I will stay only in those homes that are impure.)
Aban bhagatan sheetal bhay jaihaun.
(Now I will make your devotees feel cool/calm.)
Visphotak bhay ghor nasaihaun.
(I will destroy the terrible fear of disease.)
Shri Sheetalahin bhaje kalyana.
(Worshipping Shri Sheetala brings welfare.)
Vachan satya bhashe bhagvana.
(These words have been spoken truthfully by the Lord.)
Poojan paath Matu jab kari hai.
(When the worship and reading of the Mother are done.)
Bhay aanand sakal dukh hari hai.
(Fear vanishes, joy comes, and all sorrows are taken away.)
Visphotak bhay jihi grih bhai.
(Brother, in whichever home there is the fear of eruptive disease.)
Bhajai Devi kahin yahi upai.
(Worship the Goddess, this is the only remedy.)
Kalash Sheetala ka sajavai.
(Decorate the Kalash for Sheetala.)
Dwij se vidhivat paath karavai.
(Get the reading done methodically by a Brahmin.)
Tumhin Sheetala, jagki Mata.
(You are Sheetala, the Mother of the world.)
Tumhin pita jag ki sukhdata.
(You are the Father, the giver of happiness to the world.)
Tumhin jagaddhatri sukhsevi.
(You are the nourisher of the world, the server of happiness.)
Namo namami Sheetale Devi.
(I bow to you, O Goddess Sheetala.)
Namo sukhkarni dukh-harni.
(Salutations to the giver of happiness, the remover of sorrows.)
Namo-namo jagtarani dharni.
(Salutations, salutations to the savior of the world, the bearer.)
Namo namo trilokya vandini.
(Salutations to the one worshipped in the three worlds.)
Dukhdaridrak nikandini.
(The destroyer of sorrows and poverty.)
Shri Sheetala, Shedhala, Mahala.
(Shri Sheetala, Shedhala, Mahala.)
Runalihrinani Matru Mandala.
(The remover of debts, the Mother circle.)
Ho tum digambar tanudhari.
(You are the one clad in space/sky.)
Shobhit panchnam asavari.
(Decorated with the five-name ride.)
Rasabh, khar, Baisakh sunandan.
(The donkey ride, the auspicious one.)
Gardabh durvakand nikandan.
(The destroyer of the donkey/problems.)
Sumirat sang Sheetala mai, jahi sakal sukh door parai.
(Remembering Mother Sheetala removes all sorrows.)
Galka, galgandadi juhoi.
(Goitre and other such diseases.)
Taakar mantra na aushadhi koi.
(Have no mantra or medicine other than you.)
Ek Matu ji ka aaradhan.
(Only the worship of Mother is the way.)
Aur nahin koi hai sadhan.
(There is no other means.)
Nishchay Matu sharan jo aavai.
(Whoever comes to the Mother's refuge with certainty.)
Nirbhay man ichhit phal paavai.
(With a fearless mind, they get the desired fruit.)
Kodhi, nirmal kaya dharai.
(The leper attains a pure body.)
Andha, drig nij drishti niharai.
(The blind one regains sight in their eyes.)
Bandhya nari putra ko paavai.
(The barren woman gets a son.)
Janm daridra dhani hoi jaavai.
(The born-poor becomes wealthy.)
Matu Sheetala ke gun gaavat.
(Singing the virtues of Mother Sheetala.)
Lakha mook ko chhand banaavat.
(Even the mute person starts creating verses.)
Yaame koi karai jani shanka.
(Let no one have any doubt in this.)
Jag mein maiya ka hi danka.
(The Mother's glory reigns supreme in the world.)
Bhagat ‘Kamal’ Prabhudasa.
(The devotee 'Kamal' Prabhudasa.)
Tat Prayag se poorab pasa.
(From the east side of the Prayag bank.)
Gram Tiwari poor mam basa.
(My residence is in the village of Tiwari-pur.)
Kakra Ganga tat Durvasa.
(On the banks of the Ganga at Kakra, near Durvasa.)
Ab vilamb main tohi pukarat.
(Now without delay, I am calling you.)
Matru kripa kau baat niharat.
(Looking for the path to Mother's grace.)
Pada dwar sab aas lagayi.
(Lying at your door with all hope.)
Ab sudhi let Sheetala mai.
(Now, Mother Sheetala, please remember me/take note of me.)

|| Doha ||
Yeh Chalisa Sheetala, paath kare jo koy.
(Whoever reads this Sheetala Chalisa.)
Sapne dukh vyaape nahi, nit sab mangal hoy.
(Sorrows do not touch them even in dreams, and everything becomes auspicious.)
Bujhe sahasra vikrami shukl, bhaal bhal kintu.
(In the bright fortnight of Vikram Samvat.)
Jag janani ka ye charit, rachit bhakti ras bintu.
(This story of the Mother of the world is written with the essence of devotion.)

faq.q1: Why is the Sheetla Chalisa recited?
faq.a1: Maa Sheetla is revered as the Goddess of health and healing. The Chalisa is recited to seek protection from ailments, alleviate physical discomfort (like fever or inflammation), and safeguard the family from infectious diseases.
faq.q2: What is the most auspicious time to recite it?
faq.a2: Sheetala Ashtami is the primary festival dedicated to her. Additionally, reciting it on Mondays and Tuesdays is considered highly beneficial.
faq.q3: What is the significance of offering 'cold food' (Basoda) to Maa Sheetla?
faq.a3: Maa Sheetla is the deity of coolness. On the day of Basoda, cold food prepared the night before is offered to the Goddess, and devotees consume the same. This tradition is believed to cool the body and build immunity against diseases.
faq.q4: Are neem leaves mandatory in her worship?
faq.a4: Yes, neem is considered the abode of Maa Sheetla. Neem has potent medicinal and antiviral properties, which is why it is essential to use neem leaves while performing her rituals.
faq.q5: Does this Chalisa provide relief from fevers or physical ailments?
faq.a5: The name 'Sheetla' itself means 'the one who cools.' Regular recitation of this Chalisa is believed to help in regulating body temperature and providing relief from various physical and mental ailments by bestowing the Goddess's cooling grace.
